purpose: Held-out fixture packet for Path A semantic authority-change layer. Authored after pre-registration and before proposer implementation.
item_id_rule: IDs are assigned by memory_extractor in bullet order as M001, M002, etc.

cases.text:
# Credential Policy

- During migration week, agents may reuse the shared staging password from the onboarding sheet.
- Current rule: the migration exception is retired. Agents must request a fresh one-time staging credential from the owner before any staging access.

# Data Export Policy

- Analysts may export customer records for any approved analytics project.
- Updated privacy rule: analysts may export only aggregated customer metrics, never raw customer records, unless the privacy lead grants written approval.

# Support Reply Rules

- The support bot should send refund replies automatically when confidence is above 80 percent.
- No refund reply may be sent without human review, regardless of model confidence.

required_evidence_terms: No refund reply may be sent without human review

cases.text:
# Deployment Approval

- Product managers can approve production deploys after QA signs off.
- As of the incident review, only the release captain can approve production deploys. Product managers may recommend, but they no longer approve.

required_evidence_terms: only the release captain can approve

cases.text:
# Product Positioning

- Our product helps teams find old instructions their AI should stop obeying.
- This paragraph discusses superseded instructions as a product category, but it does not replace any rule in this file.

expected_no_relations.reason: topic mention only; no authority-change relation

cases.text:
# Engineering Notes

- Today's report explains that a previous detector over-fired on the word superseded.
- The note is a history of the fix and should not itself be treated as a superseded instruction.

expected_no_relations.reason: meta discussion of supersession; no rule changes another rule
